004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.functions;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
020
021import java.time.*;
022import java.time.format.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.*;
025
026/**
027 * Date/time functions for the {@code #{...}} script catalog.
028 *
029 * <p>
030 * All functions exchange epoch milliseconds as the integration currency. Formatting and parsing
031 * use {@link DateTimeFormatter} patterns for the explicit-pattern variants;
032 * the no-pattern variants use ISO-8601 ({@code Instant.toString()} on the way out, the ISO
033 * detection chain on the way in).
034 */
035public final class DateFunctions {
036
037   private DateFunctions() {}
038
039   /** All function classes in this category. */
040   @SuppressWarnings({
041      "unchecked", // Cast is safe: type verified by caller context.
042      "java:S2386" // ALL is an immutable compile-time registry; exposed as an array for the cross-package/varargs functions(...) API, so visibility cannot be reduced.
043   })
044   public static final Class<? extends VarFunction>[] ALL = new Class[] {
045      Now.class, ParseDate.class, FormatDate.class
046   };
047
048   /** {@code #{now()}} — current epoch milliseconds (UTC). */
049   public static class Now extends TypedFunction {
050      @Override public String name() { return "now"; }
051      public String invoke() { return String.valueOf(System.currentTimeMillis()); }
052   }
053
054   /**
055    * {@code #{parseDate(s)}} / {@code #{parseDate(s, format)}} — parses an ISO-8601 string or
056    * a {@link DateTimeFormatter}-pattern string to epoch milliseconds.
057    *
058    * <p>
059    * The single-arg form tries, in order: {@link Instant#parse}, {@link OffsetDateTime#parse},
060    * {@link ZonedDateTime#parse}, {@link LocalDateTime#parse} (assumed UTC),
061    * {@link LocalDate#parse} (start-of-day UTC).
062    */
063   public static class ParseDate extends TypedFunction {
064      @Override public String name() { return "parseDate"; }
065
066      public String invoke(String s) {
067         if (isEmpty(s)) return "0";
068         return String.valueOf(parseIso(s));
069      }
070
071      public String invoke(String s, String format) {
072         if (isEmpty(s)) return "0";
073         if (isEmpty(format)) return invoke(s);
074         var fmt = DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern(format);
075         try {
076            return String.valueOf(LocalDateTime.parse(s, fmt).toInstant(ZoneOffset.UTC).toEpochMilli());
077         } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") DateTimeParseException e) {
078            // Try as a date-only pattern.
079         }
080         return String.valueOf(LocalDate.parse(s, fmt).atStartOfDay(ZoneOffset.UTC).toInstant().toEpochMilli());
081      }
082
083      @SuppressWarnings({
084         "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity: small ISO-format dispatch.
085         "java:S1166", // Exceptions swallowed intentionally — this is a try-each-format chain.
086      })
087      private static long parseIso(String s) {
088         try { return Instant.parse(s).toEpochMilli(); } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") DateTimeParseException e) { /* fall through */ }
089         try { return OffsetDateTime.parse(s).toInstant().toEpochMilli(); } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") DateTimeParseException e) { /* fall through */ }
090         try { return ZonedDateTime.parse(s).toInstant().toEpochMilli(); } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") DateTimeParseException e) { /* fall through */ }
091         try { return LocalDateTime.parse(s).toInstant(ZoneOffset.UTC).toEpochMilli(); } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") DateTimeParseException e) { /* fall through */ }
092         return LocalDate.parse(s).atStartOfDay(ZoneOffset.UTC).toInstant().toEpochMilli();
093      }
094   }
095
096   /**
097    * {@code #{formatDate(epoch)}} — renders {@code epoch} as ISO-8601 ({@link Instant#toString()}).
098    *
099    * <p>
100    * {@code #{formatDate(epoch, format)}} — renders {@code epoch} per a
101    * {@link DateTimeFormatter} pattern in UTC.
102    */
103   public static class FormatDate extends TypedFunction {
104      @Override public String name() { return "formatDate"; }
105
106      public String invoke(long epoch) {
107         return Instant.ofEpochMilli(epoch).toString();
108      }
109
110      public String invoke(long epoch, String format) {
111         if (format == null || format.isEmpty()) return invoke(epoch);
112         var fmt = DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern(format).withZone(ZoneOffset.UTC);
113         return fmt.format(Instant.ofEpochMilli(epoch));
114      }
115   }
116}
